items.title: Full-batch vs mini-batch gradient descent
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  data["training set N examples"] --> full["full batch: all N"]
  data --> mini["mini-batch: B examples"]
  full --> gf["gradient over N"]
  mini --> gb["gradient over B"]
  gf --> step1["one update per epoch"]
  gb --> step2["N/B updates per epoch"]
items.caption: Mini-batch SGD performs many cheaper updates per epoch by estimating the gradient from B ≪ N examples each time.

items.title: One SGD training step
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  shuffle["shuffle or sample indices"] --> slice["take batch B"]
  slice --> fwd["forward pass"]
  fwd --> loss["compute batch loss"]
  loss --> back["backprop: grad w.r.t. theta"]
  back --> upd["theta <- theta - eta * grad"]
items.caption: Each SGD step: sample a batch, compute loss and gradient, update parameters with learning rate η.
